title: Competency Inventory and Independent Evidence Ledger · Opportunity Brief: Competency Behavior
summary: A candidate lists technologies and course completions, yet cannot show the behavior, changed case, personal contribution, limitation, or current reproducibility behind any claim. Predict the hiring decision, evidence quality, affected people, uncertainty, and ethical boundary before reviewing a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the competency behavior increment in competency-to-evidence ledger. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging,…
objectives: Translate course, work, community, care, creative, and self-directed experience into observable role-relevant behaviors.
Create a learner-controlled review cadence that adapts tactics from evidence without treating rejection as identity evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# job-search cumulative learner-controlled career campaign
# Browser labs review fictional evidence contracts only. They never contact people,
# submit applications, access accounts, expose personal data, negotiate, or accept offers.
workspace: career
candidate-id: fictional-learner
target-role: [replace with role family, work outcomes, and bounded level]
jurisdiction: [replace with location and current official guidance boundary]
audience: [replace with reviewer need, time, format, and access context]
claim: [replace with one truthful role-relevant behavior claim]
evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, observation, changed case, contribution, and limit]
process-state: [replace with discover, investigate, apply, interview, offer, close, or archive]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
privacy-consent: [replace with purpose, minimization, permission, retention, deletion, and safe contact]
decision: [replace with learner-owned next decision and stop rule]
transfer-boundary: learner-approved external actions only
review:

steps.title: Predict Competency Behavior · Theory Competency Behavior
steps.instruction:
theory case — A course-rich skills list cannot identify one independently reproduced behavior, changed case, personal contribution, current artifact, or honest limitation. The theory variation 2 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: defend one bounded career decision

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Competency Behavior contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: Predict Competency Behavior · Theory Competency Behavior.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for translate course, work, community, care, creative, and self-directed experience into observable role-relevant behaviors. For Predict Competency Behavior · Theory Competency Behavior, keep Competency Behavior connected to mechanically reviewable target-role, truthful-claim, contribution, accessibility, privacy, consent, process-state, changed-candidate, failure, repair, decision, and transfer evidence.

content.text: A course-rich skills list cannot identify one independently reproduced behavior, changed case, personal contribution, current artifact, or honest limitation. content.title: Commit before feedback
content.text: Choose from the stated data before opening the worked model. Record which Competency Behavior condition decided your prediction.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: EEOC Disability Discrimination and Employment Decisions — Controls U.S.-specific applicant accommodation, confidentiality, pre-offer inquiry, and employment-decision boundaries while requiring local qualified guidance elsewhere.

options.text: Translate course, work, community, care, creative, and self-directed experience into observable role-relevant behaviors. Apply it to the concrete input and state the observable result.
Only paid employment produces valid professional evidence. That claim fails the predict competency behavior evidence boundary for Competency Behavior.

For predict competency behavior, apply Competency Behavior without its stated preconditions, omit the competency-to-evidence ledger trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Competency Behavior for competency-to-evidence ledger.

content.text: Translate course, work, community, care, creative, and self-directed experience into observable role-relevant behaviors. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Name learner goal, target role, jurisdiction, audience, claim, contribution, source date, evidence revision, accessibility, privacy, consent, process state, changed candidate, rejected failure, causal repair, decision owner, non-claim, and transfer limit; then calibrate the application or career decision to that exact evidence. Apply that move specifically to Competency Behavior while producing competency-to-evidence ledger.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A course-rich skills list cannot identify one independently reproduced behavior, changed case, personal contribution, current artifact, or honest limitation. The worked example variation 2 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Translate course, work, community, care, creative, and self-directed experience into observable role-relevant behaviors. A defensible solution applies Competency Behavior, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to competency-to-evidence ledger.

content.code:
# Worked focus: career-competency-behavior — Translate course, work, community, care, creative, and self-directed experience into observable role-relevant behaviors.
workspace: career
candidate-id: fictional-candidate-4
focus: career-competency-behavior
decision-evidence: Translate course, work, community, care, creative, and self-directed experience into observable role-relevant behaviors.
target-role: accessible software quality engineer
jurisdiction: example location requiring current official review
audience: technical reviewer with ten minutes and keyboard-only access
claim: one bounded role behavior is supported by a revision-linked changed case
evidence: repository case study, exact test result, contribution note, and limitation
process-state: investigate before applying
accessibility: structured text, reflow, visible focus, plain-language status, and equivalent format
privacy-consent: publish no sensitive person or third-party data; contact only with explicit consent
decision: proceed only after identity, role, evidence, access, and risk checks pass
verify: inspect links, extraction order, revision, changed case, disclosure, and source date
failure: reject invented impact, hidden assistance, exposed data, or unverified recruiter identity
repair: correct the first unsupported claim and add a changed-candidate regression
owner: learner controls every external action
transfer-boundary: manual learner-approved profile, application, conversation, interview, and offer decisions
content.caption: Runnable transformation example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Only paid employment produces valid professional evidence. This fails the Competency Behavior contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.label: Research anchor
content.value: EEOC Disability Discrimination and Employment Decisions (U.S. federal guidance reviewed 2026-07-15) — Controls U.S.-specific applicant accommodation, confidentiality, pre-offer inquiry, and employment-decision boundaries while requiring local qualified guidance elsewhere.

content.label: Mastery evidence
content.value: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Translate course, work, community, care, creative, and self-directed experience into observable role-relevant behaviors.
